def: s.m. The knee-pan, patella.
more: n. m. Dim. khurpī , n. f. S. खउरप or क्षुरप्र 1. An instrument for scraping grass; a weeding knife; a native hoe. Khurpā toṛūṅ, jālī toṛūṅ, khodūṅ nāhīṅ ghāṅs, Sās, khasam jo kuchh kaheṅ to mār bhajāūṅ bāṅs! Rus. Wom. I'll break my spade, I'll break my net, for grass I will not ply, If spouse or mother say a word from a bamboo they shall fly. 2. A blockhead. Tū bhī nirā khurpā hai. khurpā jālī , n. m. A weeding knife and a net for holding grass, etc. Khurpā jālī kahāṅ chhoṛī?

def: s.m. An instrument for scraping or grubbing up grass, &c.; a kind of hoe, a weeding-knife; — (met.) a dolt, blockhead
s.f. A small hoe, or weeding-knife; a scraper; a small shovel (for cleaning out a bird-cage).
more: s. f. 1. A scraper, a weeding knife, the tool with which grass is scraped up for horses. 2. A shovel for cleaning out a bird-cage.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Rough, uneven, rugged, coarse; — boorish; surly, morose; — a snort; a snore; noise of one being suffocated (in these senses perhaps onomat.).

ety: old P. hūram; Zend hu+rt. ram = S. su+ram
def: adj. Pleased, glad, joyful, merry, gay, cheerful, smiling; pleasant, delightful
more: adj. 1. Pleasant, delightful. 2. Cheerful, pleased, gay, merry, glad.
subs: adj. Of a cheerful mind or disposition, glad-hearted.

def: s.f. Cheerfulness, pleasure, delight, joy, gladness, mirth.
more: s. f. Cheerfulness, pleasure, delight, gladness, gayety, mirth.

def: adj. = khurāṅṭ, q.v. adj. Very old; — experienced, knowing; crafty, cunning; — s.m. Old soil, soil trodden down by the hoofs of cattle.

ety: cf. ḵẖurram; ḵẖu = Zend hu; = S. su
def: adj. Contented, satisfied, pleased, happy, glad
subs: v.t. To make contented or happy, to please.

def: s.f. Contentment, satisfaction, happiness, pleasure, joy, gladness, mirth.

s.m. The sun ( = ḵẖẉur, ḵẖẉurshed, qq.v.). part. adj. & s.m. Eating, drinking; devouring; — eater, devourer (used in comp., e.g. ḥalāl-ḵẖor, q.v.s.v. ḥalāl; — mardum-ḵẖẉur, 'Man-devouring'); — s.m. Eating, food
s.m. Snout; proboscis of an elephant.
more: s. m. The proboscis of an elephant.

ety: inf. n. of خرج 'to go out or forth,' &c.
def: s.m. Going out or forth, issuing, issue; egress; sally, eruption; exodus; — excretion
more: n. m. 1. Issue; egress; exodus. 2. Excretion; excrement. 3. Eruption. khurūj k., Ped . v. a. To issue; sally.
s. m. Going out or forth, sally, egress, issuing, issue, eruption, excretion. خروج کرنا ḵẖurūj karnā, To sally, issue, go forth.
subs: To go out or forth, to issue, to sally, &c.

def: v.t. To scrape or grub up (grass, &c.); to stub (weeds, &c.).
s.m. A dunghill cock.
more: n. m. A cock. khurūs-i-subah , The harbinger of day; chanticleer. tāj-i-khurūs , n. m. The crest or comb of a cock.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خروس (xurōs).

def: s.m. Father-in-law.
s.m.f. Whispering
more: n. f. S. अक्षरास्फुर Whispering ( kānā-phūsī , 1). Kyā khusar phusar lagā rakkhī haī? khusar phusar k. , v. a. To whisper.
subs: To talk in a whisper, to whisper.

def: adj. & s.m. Decrepit; weak; lazy, useless, worthless; — a decrepit old man; a worthless follow; — s.f. An owl (also khusaṭ).
n. m. A decrepit old man.
To be delightful.
s.f. A cess levied to defray the expenses of a private or public rejoicing (as a marriage, the birth of a son, &c.).
more: adj. (dakh.) for خوش حال ḵẖụsh-ḥāl, q. v. under خوش ḵẖụsh.

def: (dakh.) To flatter.
s. f. Perfume, fragrance.

s.f. See s.v. ḵẖẉush. adj. Good; excellent; healthy, wholesome; flourishing, prosperous, well; — sweet, delicious; delightful, agreeable, acceptable; pleasing, pleasant; beautiful, fair, charming, elegant; amiable, affable, cheerful, glad, happy, pleased, delighted, merry, gay; content, willing
more: n. f. Perfume; odour; fragrance. khush-bū-dār , adj. Sweet-smelling; sweet-scented; odoriferous; fragrant; aromatic. khush-bayāṅ , adj. Eloquent; a good speaker; an orator. khush-bayānī , n. f. Eloquence; rhetoric; oratory. khush-poshāk , adj. 1. Well-dressed. 2. Fond of good clothes. khush-tadbīrī , Good policy. khush-tahrīr, inshā-pardāz , n. m. A good writer. khush-taqrīr ; H. baktā , n. m. Skilled in argument; eloquent. khush-hāl ; H. suchit , adj. In easy circumstances ( āsūdah ). khush-hālī , n. f. Easy circumstances; competence. khush-khabar, Poet . One who brings good news. khush-khabrī , n. f. Glad tidings; good news. khush-khirām , adj. Graceful (gait). khush-khat , Good handwriting. khush-khatī, khush-navīsī , n. f. Good penmanship; caligraphy. khush-khulq , Civilized; polite. khush-khulqī , n. f. Politeness; affability; pleasing manners. khush-khuāṅ ; Illit. khushī-khāṅ , n. …
1. adj. Sweet-smelling, fragrant, odoriferous. 2. s. f. Perfume, odour. خوشبودار ḵẖụsh-bo-dār, adj. Aromatick, odoriferous, fragrant.
subs: ḵẖosh-āb, adj. Moist, fresh, juicy, succulent, full of water; having good water (a pearl, &c.); clear, pellucid; bright, brilliant
adj. Well-proportioned, of elegant form, elegant, neat
s.f. Elegance of form or proportion
adj. Good in promising, abounding in promises
adj. & adv. Sweetly singing, tuneful, melodious, musical, sweet; — with a sweet voice, melodiously
(-ko), To prove acceptable (to), to be agreeable or pleasing (to), to please
ḵẖush-āhang, adj. & adv. Sweet-voiced, melodious; — with a sweet voice
ḵẖush-āyanda, adj. Coming agreeably or pleasantly; flattering (caresses); pleasing, agreeable, delightful, amiable, charming; grateful, pleasant, wholesome

adj. & s.m. Free to stay or depart, unrestrained; having a temporary residence; — living comfortably or at ease; — one at liberty to stay or go; — a temporary resident; one newly settled; — one who lives in comfort or at ease
s.f. Liberty to stay or go, a welcome; — the living in a state of ease or comfort, or the being enabled to live so
s.f. A light assessment, low rent
ḵẖush-bo, s.f. Fragrance, perfume, odour; — adj. = ḵẖush-bū-dār, adj. — Sweet-smelling, fragrant, odoriferous, aromatic
adj. Well-dressed; — fond of good clothes
adj. Elegantly cut or shaped, well-made
adj. Of elegant mould or form
adj. Eloquent; of winning address; skilled in argument
adj. In pleasant or easy circumstances; happy; fortunate
s.f. lit. 'Having a good mole'; a mistress, a sweetheart
adj. & s.m. Bringing good news; — one who brings good news
adj. Of graceful gait, graceful in walking
s.f. Paying ready money; purchasing in private sale; private sale; — buying cheap; a bargain
or ḵẖush-ḵẖaṣlat, or ḵẖush-ḵẖulq, or ḵẖush-ḵẖo, adj. & s.m. Of good disposition, well-bred, affable, polite; — one who has a good or affable disposition
ḵẖush-ḵẖoʼī, s.f. Pleasantness of manner, affability, politeness
s.m. A good liver, one who lives well
s.m. One who sings sweetly; one who reads well; — a pupil who pays school fees
s.f. Good eating and drinking, good living
adj. Having a pleasing or beautiful mouth
s.f. Elegance or beauty of mouth
adj. Grateful to the taste, high-flavoured, tasteful, palatable, savoury
adj. Graceful in walking or in motion
adj. Of a pleasant, or good, or bright colour
adj. Having a beautiful face, beautiful
s.m. A day of festivity or diversion, a festival, a holiday
(-se), To be in a state of pleasure, to be pleased, be happy, &c.
adj. Eloquent; of winning tongue; plausible
adj. Having a fortunate end, fortunate, happy; — ḵẖush-salīqa, adj. Of pleasant manner or method, well-bred, polite
s.f. Pleasantness of manner; goodness of method; politeness; — ḵẖush-ḵẖush-sawād, adj. Of pleasant environs
adj. Of happy destiny, fortunate, prosperous
adj. Of cheerful disposition, pleasant; jocular, merry, facetious; — ḵẖush-t̤abʻī, s.f. Cheerfulness, gaiety, pleasantness; jocularity, mirth, pleasantry
adj. Of pleasing exterior or appearance, handsome
adj. Light in hand (a horse), of easy pace, gentle, tractable
adj. & s.m. Having an easy-fitting scabbard; — a sword that comes forth from the scabbard with ease, or without being drawn (such a one is considered as the best of swords)
adj. Of proper conduct or action; of pleasant action
s.f. Propriety of action or conduct; pleasantness of action
s.m. An unconditional engagement; a sale without stipulation
adj. & s.f. Well-shaped, of elegant stature; — a sweetheart
s.f. Elegance of cut or shape
adj. Of good texture or quality
v.t. To satisfy, gratify, please, delight, rejoice, gladden, amuse
adj. & s.m. Conversible, pleasant, chatty; — an entertaining talker
adj. & s.m. Living in ease and comfort; — one who possesses a competency
s.f. Passing life in ease and comfort; good living
adj. & s.m. Eloquent; melodious; — an eloquent man
adj. Pleasant, agreeable; sweet, delicious; easy of digestion
adj. Pleasantly-seated, sitting-easily, having room enough
adj. Of happy lot or portion, fortunate, lucky
adj. Pleasant to the sight, beautiful, neat, pretty
adj. & s.m. Writing an elegant hand; — a fine writer, a calligraphist; a writing-master
adj. & adv. Happy, rejoicing, cheerful, gay; — flourishing, prosperous, in good circumstances
adj. Happy; in good or happy circumstances
(-se), To be pleased (with), be satisfied, to be glad, to rejoice (at).
ex: اس کی داڑھی سے خوشبو آ رہی تھی
us ke dāṛhī se xuśbū ā rahī thī
a pleasant-smell was coming off his beard
تیری خوشبو جنت کی ہے
terī xuśbū jannat kī hai
your fragrance is that of paradise
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خوشبو (xʷašbu). By surface analysis, خوش (xuš, “pleasant; sweet”) + بو (bū, “smell, scent”). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi خوشبو (xvśbv).

def: adj. Cheerful, gay.

ety: Pehl. khushk; Zend huska; S. śushka
def: adj. Dry; parched; withered; — pure, mere, plain, bare
more: adj. 1. Dry; parched; sapless; withered. Khushk javāb . A dry answer. 2. Bare; only. Khushk chār rupaë milte haiṅ. khushk-sālī ; W. H. sūkhā ; E. sukhār , n. f. A dry year; a year of drought, scarcity or famine. khushk h. , v. a. To be dried or parched ( sūkhnā ). khushk k. , v. a. To dry ( sukhānā ). khusk-magz , adj. Dull; dunderheaded.

adj. Dry, withered. (Sans. शुष्क ). خشک دماغ ḵẖushk-damāg̠ẖ, adj. Grieved. خشک دماغي ḵẖushk-damāg̠ẖ-ī, s. f. Grief. خشک سال ḵẖushk-sāl, A general dearth, a dry year, a drought. خشک سالي ḵẖushk-sāl-ī, s. f. Drought, sterility. خشک لب ḵẖushk-lab, adj. Dry in the lips or in the shore. خشک لبي ḵẖushk-lab-i, s. f. Dryness of the lips. خشک مغز ḵẖushk-mag̠ẖz, adj. Crack-brained, crazy, hotbrained, hotheaded. خشک مغزي ḵẖushk-mag̠ẖz-ī, s. f. Hotheadedness, madness, craziness.
subs: s.m. Dry channel or bed (of a river)
s.m. Dry year, a drought, general dearth
adj. Having dry or parched lips, dry-lipped; having a dry shore or margin
adj. Dry-brained, stupid, dull, thick-headed; crack-brained, crazy; — hot-headed
v.n. To be or become dry, to dry up.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خُشْک (xušk), from Proto-Iranian *Hhúškah, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hsúškas, from Proto-Indo-European *h₂sews- + *-kos. Doublet of سُوکھا (sūkhā, “dried up”) and شُشْک (śuśk). Cognate with Punjabi خُشْک (xuśk).

def: s.m. A dry pulāʼo, q.v.; — rice boiled plain without seasoning.
more: Mah. n. m. Rice boiled plain without seasoning ( chāṅval ). khushkā khāo , intj. Be off! out! Chal dadā! mat chheṛ! bas hai kaun woh! khushkā tu khā! Uskī khātir maiṅ dhaṛī gahrī jamāūṅ? dūr pār! Wom Rangīn. Go to! dont bother, nurse, I say, be off! who is he, pray, That I to please him should my lips paint thick? tut! hence away!

def: s.m. A dry pulāʼo, q.v.; — rice boiled plain without seasoning.
s.m. corr. of قوشخانه qush-ḵẖāna, q.v.

s.f. Dryness; dearth (occasioned by drought); dry land; high lands not flooded by water (upon which grains not requiring much moisture are cultivated); — dandriff, dry scurf; — dry flour sprinkled over and under dough, or on the board, &c. on which it is kneaded (syn. palethan)
more: n. f. 1. Dryness. 2. Dearth; scarcity. 3. Scurf (Mah. Wom. bafā ; Sant. upyāṅs ). 4. Mah. Wom. The flour sprinkled on the board, etc. on which dough is kneaded to prevent its adhering to it ( palethan ). khushkī kī rāh ; H. isthal mārg . Travelling by land; in contradistinction to travelling by water.
s. f. 1. Dryness. 2. Dearth occasioned by drought. 3. Dry land. 4. adv. By land (in opposition to تري By water).

r: ḵẖushnūd, khashnūd, khashnūt, khushnūt, khshnūt, khshnu, kshṇu
ety: old P. khashnūd or khashnūt; Pehl. khushnūt; Zend khshnūt, rt. khshnu = S. kshṇu
def: adj. Contented, satisfied, pleased, happy.
s.f. Contentment, satisfaction, pleasure.
adj. Funny, jocose.
adj. Most pleasant, &c.
(inf. n. of خشع 'to be humble'), s.f.(?) Humbling oneself, humility, humiliation.

ety: for A. خشونة, inf. n. of خشن 'to be rough,' &c.
def: s.f. Roughness, harshness, asperity, severity; rigidness, austereness, austerity; animosity; fierceness; indignation; disdain.
more: (from خشن) s. f. Asperity, harshness, rigidness, austerity, austereness, animosity, disdain, fierceness, indignation.

def: s.f. (dialec.) = khus-pus, or khusar-phusar.
s.f. (colloq.) = khusar-phusar, q.v. s.m.f. Whispering
subs: To talk in a whisper, to whisper.

r: ḵẖusrau, ḵẖusrū, khusrav, khaśrūh, husravanh, su-śravas
ety: old P. khusrav; Pehl. khaśrūh; Zend husravanh; S. su-śravas, 'famous'
def: s.m. Famous or great king; a royal surname; the celebrated king Cyrus or Chosroes; — name of a famous poet of Dehli.
more: s. m. 1. Name of a celebrated king ( Cyrus or Chosroes ). 2. A king in general. 3. Name of a most famous poet of Dihlī.

def: adj. & adv. Prince-like, princely, kingly, royal; in a princely manner; — like Ḵẖusrav.
more: adv. or adj. Princelike, kingly, royal, princely.

def: adj. Belonging to a king, royal, regal, imperial.
more: adj. Belonging to ḵẖusrau or to a king, royal, imperial, regal.

ety: inf. n. of خسف 'to become eclipsed,' &c.
def: s.m. Eclipse of the moon.
more: n. m. خسف sank, swallowed. An eclipse of the moon.
(from خسف) s. m. An eclipse of the moon.

ety: for A. خصومة, v.n. fr. iii of خصم, 'to contend or dispute'
def: s.f. Contention, altercation, litigation, quarrelling, strife; animosity, enmity
more: n. f. خصم disputed. Rare . See بير khusūmat-i-shadīd . Violent animosity.
(from خصم) s. f. Enmity, quarrelling, contention, strife.

def: s. plur. (of خصر) Middles of men, waists.
s.m. Doing anything particular; — particularity, speciality; — affair, business, thing, particular; — adv. Particularly, &c. ( = ḵẖuṣūṣaṉ)
more: (from خصّ see خاص) s. m. 1. An affair, business, thing, particular. 2. Doing any thing particular. 3. adv. Particularly.

def: adv. Particularly, especially, specially.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خصوصا (xusūsan), from Arabic خُصُوصًا (ḵuṣūṣan, “especially”).

ety: for A. خصوصية, inf. n. of خصّ
def: s.f. Particularity, peculiarity, speciality, specialty; singularity; — attachment, friendship
more: n. f. Special property; peculiarity. ba-khasūsiyat , adv. Ped. With special reference to; especially.
s. f. Peculiarity, particularity, singularity, attachment, friendship.

ety: for A. خصية ḵẖuṣyat, fem.; n. of unity, fr. خصي
def: s.m. A testicle (syn. ḵẖāya; āṅḍ): — ḵẖuṣye-bardār; and ḵẖuṣye sahlānā = ḵẖāya-bardār; and ḵẖāya sah-lānā respectively; q.v.s.v. ḵẖāya.
more: n. m. A testicle ( āṅḍ ). khusye-bardār , E. n. m. lit . employed to carry another's testicles. See khāyā bardār. khusye sahlānā , Slang. v. a. To lick the dust; to fawn on one.

ety: Prk. खुट्ट˚; S. खुड, prob. a Prk. form of खण्ड
def: s.f. Pecking, tapping; peck; tap (used in comp.; cf. khuṭak)

s.m. (dialec.) = khūṅṭ, q.v. s.m. Part, side, quarter, direction; station; corner, angle; pivot, central point; wheeling point (in military evolution); — portion, share; — ear-wax; — cake offered to a goddess; — adv. In the direction of, towards
subs: or ćau-khūṅṭ, s.m. The four corners or quarters of the world.

def: s.m. = khūṅṭā, q.v. s.m. A stump; a stake (of wood), a pile; a tent-peg; a peg, pin; a pivot; the wooden handle of a quern or handmill; the upright post in a sugar-mill; — (met.) support, protection
subs: (-ko), 'To beat against a peg or stake;' to spurn, scorn
'To skip or jump to the extent of one's tether'; to presume on the protection of (another)
(-ko), 'To tie (one) to a butcher's stake or peg'; to expose (one) to danger; to put (one) in an unpleasant or trying situation; to confound; — to marry (a girl) to a cruel person.
wety: From Sanskrit कौट (kauṭa).

def: s.m. pl. (of ḵẖat̤īb), Preachers.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic خُطَبَاء (ḵuṭabāʔ).

ety: khoṭā, q.v.+āʼī, as in khaṭāʼī, q.v.
def: s.f. Faithlessness, dishonesty, insincerity, perfidy, deceit; want of virtue or merit; vice, wickedness, villainy; malice, spite, rancour; — spuriousness; adulteration, alloy; defect, flaw, blemish (syn. khoṭā-pan).
wety: From Prakrit अइआ, from Sanskrit ता (tā) + इका (-ikā).

def: s.f. Pecking, tapping
prop. n. Tartary; a district of Tartary (famous for musk).
more: n. prop. A country in Turkistān, Tartary or the north of China, famous for musk.

def: s.m. = khoṭā-pan, and khuṭāʼī, qq.v. Baseness (of coin), adulteration, alloy; flaw; corruption; deceit, fraud, falseness, &c. &c. (see khoṭā, and khuṭāʼī).
s. m. (dakh.) See کهوٿائي khoṭā-ī.

ety: for A. خطبة ḵẖut̤bat, fem.; v.n. fr. خطب 'to recite'
def: s.m. A discourse, sermon, oration, or harangue which the ḵẖat̤īb or preacher recites on the pulpit in the noon-service of the congregational mosque on Friday (it consists of expressions of praise to God, exhortation, blessings on Mohammad and his family and companions, and prayer for the Mohammadans in general, and especially for the reigning sovereign)
more: n. m. A public prayer for the reigning king offered up at the Mosques at the Friday service and on special occasions. khutbah-khuāṅ, khatīb , n. m. One who reads the khutbah .
s. m. A sermon or oration delivered after divine service every Friday, in which the preacher blesses Muhammad, his successours, and the reigning sovereign.
subs: s.m. Reciter of a ḵẖut̤ba (see ḵẖat̤īb).
wety: From Arabic خُطْبَة (ḵuṭba) (through Classical Persian خطبه (xut̤ba)).

def: s.m. See s.v. khuṭ. s.f. Pecking, tapping; peck; tap (used in comp.; cf. khuṭak)
s.f. = khūṭhī, q.v.
more: s. f. A scab. See کهوٿهي.

def: s.f. Scab, scale, cicatrice (cf. khūthī).
s.f. = khūṅṭī, q.v. s.f. A peg, pin, cog; short stubble; very short hair.

ety: corr. fr. P. kutk, or kūtak
def: s.m. A stick, a club, a bludgeon (syn. soṅṭā; cf. gadkā); — the penis.
more: n. m. 1. A club; stick. 1. Kūṅdī ke naqāre par khutke kā lagā ḍaṅkā, Nit bhaṅg pī aur āshiq din rāt bajā ḍaṅkā . Nazīr. Pound, pound the spirit-giving bhang , drum drum away, Drink ever bhang , play on, make merry while you may. 2. Khutke par sonā! Prov. His sticks are golden. (He rolls in wealth). 2. ( Slang ) The penis. Mere pās kyā khuttak dharā hai . I have nothing.
s. m. 1. A club. 2. Penis. (Corrupt. of کتک kutak: Rashīdī).

ety: khūṭ˚ or khoṭ˚ = Prk. खुट्ट(इ) = S. क्षोट्य(ते), pass. (used actively) of rt. क्षोट्
def: v.t. To pick, pluck, gather (leaves, vegetables, or fruit, &c.); to pull up or out.
more: v. a. To pluck, to pick the leaves, &c. of vegetables.

ety: corr. fr. P. kutk, or kūtak
def: s.m. A stick, a club, a bludgeon (syn. soṅṭā; cf. gadkā); — the penis.
more: (corrupt. of کتک kutak, see ختکا) s. A club or stick.

def: s.f. corr. of qut̤t̤āma, q.v. s.f. Food, aliment, nutriment, victuals, provisions, sustenance, livelihood
more: n. f. Cor. of A. qattāmā , lustful. An unchaste, bad woman. (An abuse).
subs: Food sufficient to keep body and soul together
To manage to live, to exist.

def: s.f. Scale, scab (of a healing sore).
n. m. The scab of a healing sore.

ety: prob. S. कोष्ठ+इका; or Prk. खुट्ट˚ = S. खुड or खण्ड+इका
def: s.f. Treasure, hoard, store; ready money, cash.
more: s. f. 1. A treasure, hoard. 2. (for کٿکي kuṭkī, q. v.) A gnat.

def: s.f. A gnat ( = kuṭkī).
s.f. A purse; money-bag; — treasure; wealth; hoard.
adj. (Dakkhinī) Ashamed, abashed.

ety: inf. n. of خطر; see ḵẖat̤ar
def: s.m. Occurring to the mind, &c. ( = ḵẖat̤ar); — dignity.
more: (from خطر) s. m. 1. Coming into the mind, recollection, remembrance. 2. Dignity.

def: s.m. Parallel lines, parallels.
more: s. plur. of خط ḵẖat̤t̤, q. v.

def: adj. Linear (demonstration or measurement).
v.t. To cause to loose; to cause to part with or from (-se), cause to get rid of; to deprive of (-se).
s.m. Established order; mode, manner; — strengthening food; — a frame for turning an arch upon; a scaffold.
(for خودزاذي from خود Self, and زاده Born) adj. f. Undressed, unadorned (a woman).

ety: inf. n. of خضع 'to be humble,' &c.
def: s.f. (?), Humbling oneself; humility, lowliness ( = ḵẖushūʻ).

r: ḵẖẉāb, ḵẖāb, ḵẖvāb, qafna, swapna, kam-ḵẖẉāb, kim-ḵẖẉāb, ḵẖẉāb-ālūda
ety: Pehl. ḵẖvāb; Zend qafna; S. swapna
def: adj. Sleepy, drowsy
more: n. m. خوابيدن to sleep. 1. Sleep. 2. Dream ( supnā ); vision. khuāb-ālūdah , adj. Drowsy; drooping; languishing. khuāb-āvar ; H. nīṅd-lāū , adj. Soporific. khuāb-i-khargosh, lit . The sleep of a hare. 1. Light sleep with fits and starts. 2. Negligence; forgetfulness. khuāb dekhnā , v. a. To dream; dream a dream. khuāb-gāh , n. f. Sleeping-apartment; bed room. khuāb o khayāl ; H. supne kī māyā , n. m. Vision; reverie; illusion; phantom. khuāb o khayāl , Pop. khuāb-khiyāl , adj. Shadowy; unsubstantial; unreal; imaginary. khuāb o khayāl h. , v. n. To vanish like a dream.

s. m. f. 1. Sleep. 2. A dream. 3. Nap (of cloth). خواب آلوده ḵẖẉāb-ālūda, adj. Sleepy. خواب خيال ḵẖẉāb-ḵẖiyāl, s. m. Spectre, phantom, delusion. خواب ديکهنا ḵẖẉāb dekhnā, To dream. خوابگاه ḵẖẉāb-gāh, Sleeping-apartment, bed-room, dormitory. خواب ناک ḵẖẉāb-nāk, adj. Sleepy, drowsy.
subs: adj. & s.m. Producing sleep; — (in Med.), a soporific, anodyne
s.m. Vision, spectre, phantom, delusion; — adj. Visionary, imaginary, delusive
To have a dream, to dream
q.v.; — ḵẖẉāb-o-ḵẖayāl honā, To vanish like a vision or spectre.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خْوَاب (xwāb), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *swap-, from Proto-Indo-European *swep- (“to dream”). Doublet of سپنا (sapnā).

def: s. f. Sleeping-apartment, bed-room, dormitory.

ety: perf. part. of ḵẖẉābīdan; see ḵẖẉāb
def: part. adj. Sleeping, asleep; sleepy, drowsy.

ety: act. part. of خواستن, see ḵẖẉāst
def: part. adj. Wishing, willing, desiring, soliciting, requiring, wanting (used in comp., e.g. taraqqī-ḵẖẉāh, 'Wishing for increase, soliciting advancement'; — ḵẖāt̤ir-ḵẖẉāh, q.v.s.v. ḵẖāt̤ir
more: part. act. خواستن to desire. ( In comp .) Desiring; requiring; demanding. taraqqī-khuāh , Wishing increase or advancement. khātir-khuāh , adv. Satisfactorily; fully. daulat-khuāh . Wishing prosperity or good fortune. khuāh , Conj. Either; or; whether ( chāho ). Khuāh tum āo, khuāh woh āëṅ .

1. part. act. (of خواستن) in compos. Wishing, requiring, soliciting, desiring; as, ترقيخواه taraḳḳī-ḵẖẉāh, Wishing increase. دولتخواه daulat-ḵẖẉāh, Wishing prosperity. 2. conj. Either, or, whether. 3. part. pass. as, خاطرخواه ḵẖāt̤ir-ḵẖẉāh, The desire of oneʼs heart. خواه مخواه ḵẖẉāh ma-ḵẖẉāh or خواه نخواه ḵẖẉāh na-ḵẖẉāh or خواهي نه خواهي ḵẖẉāhī na ḵẖẉāhī, adv. 1. Willing or not willing, nolens volens. 2. Certainly, positively, absolutely, at all events.
subs: ḵẖẉāh na-ḵẖwāh, adv. Willing or unwilling, nolens volens, perforce; certainly, positively, absolutely, inevitably, at all events); — conj. Either, or; whether
ex: خواہ کچھ بھی ہو جائے
xāh kuch bhī ho jā'e
No matter what happens (lit. Even if anything happens)
خواہ یہ ہو خواہ نا ہو
xāh ye ho xāh nā ho
Whether it happens or not
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خواه (xʷāh, “willing, desiring”).

def: adj. & s.m. Wishing, desiring; desirous; — desirer, seeker
more: part. act. (of خواستن) Wishing; (adj. or sub.) Desirous, desirer, seeker.

def: s.m. One who wills, or wishes, or asks, or wants; wisher, asker, &c.
s.f. A sister.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خْوَاهَر (xwāhar).

def: adj. & adv. Sister-like, sisterly.
s.f. Wish, desire, will, inclination; request, demand
more: (from خواستن) s. f. 1. Desire, wish, will, inclination. 2. Request, demand خواهش رکهنا ḵẖẉāhish rakhnā or خواهش کرنا ḵẖẉāhish karnā, To wish, to desire. خواهش مند ḵẖẉāhish-mand, adj. Desirous.
subs: ḵẖẉāhish karnā (-kī), To have a desire (for), to desire, wish (for)
(-kī), To be in request or demand.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خواهش (xwāhiš).

def: s.m. Lord, master, owner; a man of distinction, a respectable man, a gentleman; — a rich merchant; — a eunuch (vulg. ḵẖojā)
more: n. m. A master; a man of distinction; a rich or respectable man; an opulent merchant; a gentleman. khuājah-sarā . 1. A eunuch, usually employed in the charge of a seraglio. 2. A butler; chief of a household.

s. m. A man of distinction, a rich merchant, a gentleman, a governour. خواجه خضر ḵẖẉāja ḵẖiẓr, n. prop. The name of a prophet skilled in divination, and who is said to have discovered the water of life; hence, he is considered as the saint of waters. The Muhammadans offer to him oblations of lamps, flowers, &c. placed on little rafts and launched on the river, particularly on Thursday evening in the month of bhādoṅ: and, it is in his honour that the feast of the beṛa, q. v. is held. خواجه زاده ḵẖẉāja-zāda, s. m. Master. خواجه سرا ḵẖẉāja-sarā, s. m. An eunuch.
subs: Name of a prophet skilled in divination, and who is said to have discovered and drank of the fountain of life; hence he is considered as the saint of waters (Mohammadans offer to him oblations of lamps, flowers, &c., placed on little rafts and launched on the river, particularly on Thursday evening in the month of bhādoṅ; and it is in his honour that the feast of beṛā (q.v.) is held. Travellers by boat always invoke his aid on starting, with the words Ai ḵẖẉāja ḵẖiẓr beṛā pār! He is by some confounded with the prophet Elias, and is regarded by others as a companion of Moses, or as Phineas)
s.m. A eunuch in the service of a king or prince who has free ingress to all parts of the palace, or one who has charge of the seraglio; — chief of a household; a major-domo, a butler.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خواجه (xʷāja).

def: s.m. A tray (cf. ḵẖāna)
more: n. m. A tray. khuān posh , n. m. A cloth covering over a tray; a tray cover.

1. s. m. A tray. خوانچه ḵẖẉāncha, s. m. A small tray. خوان پوش ḵẖẉān-posh, s. m. A cloth for covering a tray, a tray lid. 2. part. act. (from خواندن) used in compos. Reading, singing, repeating, reader.
subs: s.m. A cloth for covering a tray; a tray cover or lid.

ety: rt. of ḵẖẉān-dan; Pehl. qan-tan; Zend qan; S. swan
def: part. adj. & s.m. Reading, reciting, singing, chanting; — reader, reciter; chanter, &c. (used in comp., e.g. ḵẖẉush-ḵẖẉān, 'reading well')
s.m. A reader, reciter, repeater; singer, chanter; crier, convoker.
more: part. pres. used substantively, A reader.

def: s.m. A small tray; a small cash-box.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خوانچه (xʷānča).

def: s.m. (?), Reading, reciting, &c. (see ḵẖẉān).

ety: perf. part. of ḵẖẉāndan; and = S. svanita; see ḵẖwān
def: part. adj. Read, recited, repeated; — having the knowledge of reading and writing, lettered
more: adj. Ped. for H. paṛhā . 1. Lettered; being able to read and write; educated, so called. 2. Adopted (a child). nā-khuāṅdah , adj. Ped. for H. an-paṛh, ku-paḍh . Illiterate; hence, uneducated; ignorant. khuāṅdgī ; Pop. khāngī , n. f. Old lessons ( āmokhtah ).
part. pass. (of خواندن) Read; (adjectively) Having the knowledge of reading and writing. نا خوانده nā-ḵẖẉānda, adj. Ignoramus, one who cannot read.
subs: adj. & s.m. Illiterate, ignorant; — one who cannot read, an ignoramus.

def: s.f. Reading, recital; repetition (as of old lessons, &c.).
s.f. Reading, recitation, repetition (used as last member of compounds).
more: (in compos.) s. f. Reading, repeating, recitation.

def: s. m. (dakh.) A caterer ( Harris ).
s.m. = ḵẖān-sāmān, q.v. (ḵẖān+sāmā n) s.m. lit. 'Master of the stores,' a house steward, butler, major-domo.

ety: rt. Zend qar = S. svar; cf. ḵẖẉārī
def: adj. Poor, distressed; deserted, abandoned, friendless, wretched, ruined; abject, vile, base, contemptible.
more: adj. See āvārah . Lauṅḍe kā yār sadā khuār, ṭāṭ kā bichhaunā, jūtiyoṅ kā hār . Prov. The sodomite's a mean condition is, on bedding vile he lays, of shoes composed a necklace his.

1. adj. Poor, distressed, deserted, abandoned, friendless, wretched, contemptible, abject, ruined. 2. part. act. (from خواردن) in compos. Eating, devouring, eater, drinking, drinker; suffering, sufferer.

ety: act. part. of ḵẖẉārdan; rt. Zend qar; S. svad
def: part. adj. & s.m. Eating, devouring; drinking; suffering; — eater; drinker; sufferer (used in comp., e.g. mardum-ḵẖẉār, 'man-eating'; ḵẖūn-ḵẖẉār, 'blood-drinking, blood-thirsty').
adj. Worthy; fit, proper, capable, suitable; agreeable (to).

r: ḵẖẉar, ḵẖẉur, ḵẖor, khvar, hvare, svar
ety: Pehl. khvar; Zend hvare; S. svar
def: s.m. (orig.), 'Light, splendour'; the sun; the East.
adj. Worthy, &c. ( = ḵẖẉar, q.v.). adj. Poor, distressed; deserted, abandoned, friendless, wretched, ruined; abject, vile, base, contemptible.
more: part. (from خوردن) Eating, suffering; adj. Voracious, fit, capable.

ety: Zend qāiri, rt. qar = S. svar
def: s.f. Contemptibleness, meanness, baseness, vileness; abjectness, friendlessness, wretchedness, distress.
more: n. f. 1. Baseness; meanness; vileness; abjectness. 2. Difficulty; hardship. 3. Fighting; quarrel. Lāvegā dārā to khāvegī dārī, na lāvegā dārā to paṛegī khuārī . Prov. What the husband brings home the wife's free to eat, if he brings nothing home hard words will him greet. khuārī k. , v. a. 1. To expose one. 2. To fight or quarrel with; fall out. Ab-ke jo bole to bole saiyāṅ, Jo abke bole to karūṅgī khuārī, Mo se nāhīṅ bolo, chhailā, main binti kar hārī! I tell you, husband, what you have said you have said, Say it again—disgrace falls on your head, Dont speak to me, my beau, I humbly plead.
1. s. f. Baseness, meanness, contemptibleness, vileness, abjectness, distress. 2. in compos. Eating, drinking; suffering.

def: s.f. Eating; drinking; suffering (used in comp., e.g. ḵẖūn-ḵẖẉārī, 'blood-thirstiness).

ety: v.n. of ḵẖẉāstan, 'to wish'; rt. Zend qād?; S. śvas
def: s.f. Desire, wish; solicitation, request
more: (from خواستن) s. f. Desire, wish, request. خواستگار ḵẖẉāst-gār, s. m. Candidate, competitor, bidder. خواستگاري ḵẖẉāst-gār-ī, s. f. Competition, candidateship, desire.
subs: s.m. Petitioner, applicant, candidate, competitor, bidder, suitor
s.f. Desire, solicitation, &c. ( = ḵẖẉāst); candidateship; competition; a matrimonial suit.

ety: perf. part. of ḵẖẉāstan; see ḵẖẉāst
def: part. Desired, wished, willed; wished for, wanted; having wished or willed.
more: adj. ( In Comp .) See chītā. Khuāsta-i-Khudā yūṅ thā . God willed it so.
(from خواستن) part. pass. Desired, wished for, having willed or wished.

def: s.m. Wisher; asker, petitioner.

ety: Zend qae, fr. qa+pron. suff. ش; S. sve, fr. sva
def: pron. & s.m. Self; own; — a kinsman; a son-in-law; a family; — ḵẖẉesh-qarābāt, s.m. pl. Kinsmen, relations
more: adj. pron. Own; self ( apnī , 1.). Avval khesh, bādhu darvesh . Prov. First your own, then the beggar. (Charity begins at home). khesh , n. m. A kinsman; a son-in-law. khesh o aqārib , n. m. Kith and kin.
1. pron. Self, own. 2. s. m. A kinsman. 3. A son-in-law, a family. خويش قرابات ḵẖẉesh ḳarābāt, Kinsmen, relations.
subs: s.m. Kith and kin; kinsmen, &c. ( = ḵẖẉesh-qarābāt).

ety: see ḵẖẉesh; — wand, prob. = S. vant
def: s.m. A kinsman, a relative.
s.f. = ḵẖweshī, q.v. s.f. Relationship, consanguinity, affinity.
s.f. Relationship, consanguinity, affinity.

r: ḵẖẉud, ḵẖud, qae, qa, ta, ti, tu, sve, sva
ety: Zend qae, rt. qa+ta, or ti, or tu; S. sve, fr. sva+ta, &c.
def: pron. Self (my, thy, &c.); — own, private, personal
more: adj. Self ( āp ); private ( khāngī ); own; personal; in person. khud apnī zāt se , adv. Of one's own self; independently of. khud-ārāī , n. f. Self-decoration; man-millinery. khud-intizāmī . Self-government. khud-ba-khud ; H. apne āp , adv. 1. Spontaneously; of itself; naturally. 2. Voluntarily; of one's own accord. khud-ba-daulat . Yourself; your worship; your honor, etc.; sir; His majesty; we. khud-bīn , adj. Self-conceited; conceited; vain. khud-bīnī , n. f. Self-conceit; vanity; pride. khud-parast , adj. Selfish; self-indulgent. khud-parastī , n. f. The worship of self. khud-pasand , adj. Self-satisfied; self-complacent. khud pasandī , n. f. Self-esteem; self-approbation. khud-hākimī . 1. Self-government. 2. Arrogation of authority. khud-rāë , adj. 1. Self-opinionated. 2. Wilful; wayward. khud-rāī , n. f. Self-opinionatedness; wilfulness; obstinacy. khud-rāī se , adv. 1. Dogmatically…
subs: adj. Adorning or decorating self; setting off self; conceited, proud, arrogant
s.f. Self-decoration; the setting off of self, pride, &c.
adv. Of oneself; of himself (themselves, &c.), of one's own accord, voluntarily, freely; of itself, spontaneously, naturally
Yourself, your worship, your honour, your majesty; — Sir; his honour, his majesty, &c.
adj. 'Regarding self'; self-conceited, vain, proud, arrogant, presumptuous
adj. 'Worshipping self'; self-opinionated, dogmatic; self-conceited; self-indulgent, selfish
s.f. Doggedness in one's own opinion; self-sufficiency; self-conceit; self-indulgence
adj. 'Self-approved'; self-satisfied, self-complacent, vain, conceited, opiniated
adj. Self-possessing, being master of oneself; content; patient
adj. Self-opinionated; self-conceited; wilful, headstrong, wayward
s.m. One who leaves a service of his own accord; — s.f. Leaving a service of one's own account (in which case it is customary to deduct something from wages)
ḵẖud-ro or rau, adj. Growing of itself, wild, spontaneous
adj. Hurtful, or doing mischief to, oneself
adj. Self-conceited; acting of self; wilful, head-strong, obstinate; absolute, independent; arrogant, refractory
s.f. Self-conceit; self-will, obstinacy; absoluteness, independence, arrogance
adj. & s.m. 'Selling oneself'; boasting, vaunting; boastful; — a boaster, bragger, blusterer; an egotist
adj. & s.m. Cultivating one's own field; — a cultivating proprietor, a resident cultivator; — one's own cultivation, land which the proprietor or holder cultivates himself
adj. Following one's own desire or will, self-willed, selfish; living like a libertine
s.f. Self-will; selfishness, wilfulness; the persisting in what is pleasing to self
part. adj. & s.m. Made or done of self; — one's own act or deed
s.f. Self-destruction, suicide; — ḵẖud-kushī karnā, To commit suicide
adj. & s.m. Independent, unrestrained, uncontrolled, free; — a free agent
s.m. A dervish on his own account, one who acknowledges no saint or pīr
ex: میں یہ کام خود کروں گا۔
ma͠i ye kām xud karūṅgā.
I will do this work myself.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خْوَد (xwad)/خْوُد (xwud).

def: recip. pron. plur. (of ḵẖẉud), Selves; themselves.
s.f. Self; identity; — selfishness, egotism, conceit, vanity, pride, arrogance
more: n. f. 1. Self; one's self; personal identity; selfishness; egotism; pride; self-conceit; arrogance. Khudī aur Khudāī meṅ bair hai . Prov. Self is at enmity with God. be-khudī , n. f. 1. Being beside oneself; madness; transport. 2. Perfect freedom or independence; sans soucī. Maë se /?/garaz nishāt hai kis rū-siyāh ko? Ek gūnā be-khudī mujhe din rāt chāhīye . Gālib. What black-faced wretch is there would drink for pleasure's sake? My soul to free all night and day my glass I take.
subs: s.f. The state of being beside ooneself; ecstasy, transport; madness; — independence, freedom.

def: adj. Abject, despicable, &c. ( = ḵẖẉār, q.v.)
subs: s.m. Dirty or impure water; — a floodgate, dam of a sluice.

ety: act. part. of ḵẖẉurdan; rt. Zend qar; S. svad
def: part. adj. & s.m. Eating, drinking; devouring; — eater, devourer (used in comp., e.g. ḥalāl-ḵẖor, q.v.s.v. ḥalāl; — mardum-ḵẖẉur, 'Man-devouring'); — s.m. Eating, food
more: adj. P. خوردن to eat. ( In comp .) Eating; drinking; devouring. khur o posh, nān o nafqah ; H. roṭī kapṛā , Food and raiment; bed and board. khur o nosh ; H. khānā pīnā , n. f. Meat and drink.
1. adj. Worthy. 2. s. m. The sun. 3. or ḵẖor, Food, eating. 4. part. act. (from خوردن) in compos. Eating, drinking, eater, drinker, feeder.

def: s.m. Anything that eats or corrodes, &c. (as rust, moth, gangrene, &c.); the leprosy; — that which, or one who, suffers or endures (used in comp.).
more: in compos. (dakh.) Who has endured or suffered.
s. m. What eats or corrodes, as rust, &c.

def: adj. Eating; experiencing, suffering; — voracious.
s.f. Food, victuals, provisions; eatables; daily food, rations, diet, board, regulated allowance of food; — one meal; one dose (of medicine, &c.)
more: n. f. 1. Daily food, rations, diet, or allowance; regulated allowance. 2. Food; victuals; provisions. 3. One dose (of medicine, etc). khūrāk-i-afyāl , 1. Food of elephants. 2. A tax formerly levied by the Mugal emperors. khūrāk'ī ; H. peṭyā , n. f. Daily allowance of food, or money to purchase it; subsistence money. khūrākī ; H. chārū , adj. Having a good appetite; eating much.
s. f. (dakh. s. m.) 1. Food, victuals. 2. Daily food, eatables. 3. One meal, one dose (of medicine).
subs: s.f. Food for elephants; a tax formerly levied by the Mogal emperors.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خْوَرَاک (xwarāk). First attested in c. 1609 as Middle Hindi خوراک (xvrak)

def: s.f. Daily allowance of food, or of money to purchase it; subsistence money, board-wages; — adj. & s.m. Eating much, gormandizing: — a gourmand.
more: s. f. Daily allowance of food or of money to purchase it, subsistence-money, board-wages.

def: s.m. An eater, feeder; a great eater, gormandizer.
more: part. act. (from خوردن) used substantively, An eater, feeder, gormandizer.

def: adj. = خرد ḵẖurd, q.v. adj. Small, little, minute; young
more: 1. adj. Small. خورد سال ḵẖụrdsāl, adj. Young, of tender years. خورد سالي ḵẖụrd-sāl-ī, Youth. خورد محل ḵẖụrd-maḥall, s. m. The apartments of the concubines of great men. 2. (from خوردن) verbal noun, Eating, drinking.
subs: adj. Of tender years, young; under age
s m. The apartments of the concubines of great men.

r: ḵẖẉurd, ḵẖẉurdan, ḵẖẉur, ḵẖẉurda, ḵẖẉurd-burd, lit, ḵẖẉurd-burd karnā
def: v.t. To make away with, to consume, to spend, to appropriate; to embezzle.
part. Eaten, devoured, consumed; taken, appropriated, made away with (often contrac. to ḵẖẉurd); — past conj. part. Having eaten, &c.; — part. adj. Afflicted; corroded, &c.; — s.m. for ḵẖurda, q.v.
more: (contracted خورد ḵẖụrd ) part. pass. (of خوردن) 1. Eaten, taken, having eaten or taken. 2. (adjectively) Afflicted. 3. s. m. A blemish. خورده بين ḵẖụrda-bīn, s. m. A caviller, a criticiser. خورده پکڙنا ḵẖụrda pakaṛrnā, To criticise. خورده فروش ḵẖụrda-farosh, s. m. A retailer, a huckster. خورده فروشي کرنا ḵẖụrda-farosh-ī karnā, To retail. خورده کرنا ḵẖụrda karnā, To change money. خورده گير ḵẖụrda-gīr, A criticiser, caviller.

def: s.f. Small change, &c. ( = ḵẖurda, q.v.). s.m. Anything small, a small qnantity, a bit, fragment, fraction, a particle; a crumb; a speck; a slight blemish; a pedlar's small or trifling wares; small coin, small change
subs: v.t. To sell in small quantities or in retail
adj. & s.m. Acute; hypercritical; — a critic, a caviller
s.f. Subtilty of genius, acuteness; hypercriticism
To seize on trifling faults, to cavil, to criticise
s.m. A vender of small wares, a pedlar, a huckster
v.t. To reduce to small pieces; — to change (money); — to sell, dispose of
adj. & s.m. Fault-finding, fault-seeking; cavilling; — a caviller, a critic
s.f. Seizing on trifling faults, cavilling

def: inf. n. m. Eating; drinking; devouring; suffering.
adj. & s.f. Fit to be eaten, eatable; — anything eatable; provisions.

def: s.m. more: (Fac.) qabīlah wife, n. m. قبل before. 1. The temple of the kābah in Mecca. 2. The direction in which Moslems look when praying. 3. Any august, venerable, or reverend person, as a father, king, sire, patron. 1. Āiye qabilā ( qiblah )! Mazāq. Jest. Come, wife (Sire)! 2. Qiblah, āp kyā farmāte haiṅ? qibla-i-hājāt . One who supplies another's needs. qibla-i-ālam ; Pop. qiblā ālam , The pole star of the world, a title of Eastern monarchs; His or your majesty. qibla-i-kaunain . The qiblah of both worlds, applied to a father. qiblah-gāh, vālid mājid , n. m. 1. A father. 2. ( Slang ) A superior ( abbā , 2). Tumhāre qiblah-gāh ko to detā hī nahīṅ . I would n't give it up to your father (much less to you)!

s. m. 1. Any thing opposite. 2. That part to which people turn their face when at prayer; (hence, the word means) Mecca, an altar, temple; (and, by way of respectful address) Worship! Father! &c. قبلهٴحاجات ḳibla-i-ḥājāt, What is looked to for the attainment of necessities or desires. قبلهٴعالم ḳibla-e-āﻌlam, The ḳibla of the world, his Majesty, your Majesty! قبله کونين ḳibla-e-kaunain, The ḳibla of both worlds, applied to a father. قبله گاه ḳibla-gāh, The place turned to when at prayer, generally means a father or one beloved. قبله نما ḳibla-numā ( lit. shewing the ḳibla or direction of Mecca) A marinerʼs compass, an instrument to ascertain the direction of Mecca.

more: v. a. 1. To pin or nail together. Ādhā bājā, ādhā gīlā, sabhoṅ ko le ek hī meṅ kīlā . Conundrum. Ans. Daftar. ( Daf a drum, tar wet. Daf-tar . An Office). 2. To conjure; charm. 1. Iskā muṅh kīl do! 2. Faqīr kī zabān kis ne kīlī hai? Prov. Who can stop the medicants tongue? 3. To destroy; stop the propagation of by magic. Chīṅṭiyāṅ kīl dīṅ . 4. To shut up; silence. Muṅh kīlnā .
v. a. To charm a snake so as to prevent his biting.

def: The formative pl. of the interrog. pron. kaun (cf. kis).
conj. Which; for, because; that this.
v.t. To do, make, &c. ( = karnā, q.v.). s.f. A musical horn; the large brass trumpet which sounds the bass; a trumpet, bugle, clarion, &c.
more: v. a. To do, to make. See کرنا karnā.

ety: Pehl. kīn; Zend kaêna, fr. ći = S. चि
def: s.m. Hatred, rancour, malevolence, malice, spite, grudge, resentment, animosity (syn. bugẓ; khuns)
more: n. m. Rancour (بغض). kīne se . Feloniously; maliciously. kīna-var, kīna-toz , adj. Malicious; rancorous.

s. m. Malice, spite, rancour, resentment, hatred, grudge. کينه ور kīna-war, adj. Malicious, spiteful, rancorous. کينه جوٴي kīna-jo-ī, s. f. Seeking malice or hatred. کينه کش kīna-kash, adj. Spiteful, malicious, revengeful.
subs: or kīna-ḵẖẉāh, adj. & s.m. = kīna-war, q.v.
or kīna-war, adj. & s.m. Malicious, rancorous, revengeful, spiteful; inimical; — a malicious person, &c.
or kīna-warī, s.f. Animosity, rancour, malignity, &c.

def: v. n. To strain while evacuating.
s.f. Chinchina, Peruvian bark.
s.m. = next, q.v.
more: (or prop. kanāra ) s. m. Side, margin, shore, strand, bank (of a river, &c.), beach, border, coast, part, limit, boundary, edge. کنارا پکڙنا kinārā pakaṛnā, To retire. کنارا کرنا kinārā karnā, v. a. To abstain, to refrain, to keep aloof, retire or withdraw (from), to lay aside. کناره کش kināra-kash, Withdrawing. کناري kināre or کناري پر kināre par, Aside, ashore. کناري چڙهنا kināre chaṛhnā, v. n. To run ashore, to strand. کناري هوجانا kināre ho-jānā, To retire, to withdraw.

def: s.f. Edging, border, trimming, lace (of gold or silver)
more: n. f. Gold or silver lace; edging; border. Jānī! torā dhotyā meṅ lāgalbā kenārī . Bhoj. song. kinārī-bāf , n. m. A lace-maker.

def: s.m. = P کنايت kināyat, s.f. (for A. کناية kināyat, inf. n. of کني 'to call by a significant name'), Allusion; metaphor, trope, figure, metonymy; a nickname; an ironical expression; innuendo; sarcasm, taunt, jeer, banter; a wink, nod, hint, sign (syn. ishāra).
more: (from کنا Heb. ) s. m. Allusion, metaphor, trope, an ironical expression, sarcasm, a wink, nod, hint, sign. کنايةً kināyataṉ, adv. By allusion, allusively, metaphorically.

def: s. f. See کنايه kināya.

s.f. = P کنايه kināya, s.m. (for A. کناية kināyat, inf. n. of کني 'to call by a significant name'), Allusion; metaphor, trope, figure, metonymy; a nickname; an ironical expression; innuendo; sarcasm, taunt, jeer, banter; a wink, nod, hint, sign (syn. ishāra).

ety: for A. کنايةً, acc. of kināyat
def: adv. By allusion, indirectly; metaphorically, figuratively; enigmatically; by way of innuendo; by signs, or winks, &c.
more: adv. Indirectly; as an inunedo. kināyatan manā k. , v. a. To forbid by winks.

def: v. n. To sprinkle. Chūran khāte nārī nal, is meṅ kinchhā Gangā jal . Street cry. Digestives, men and women, buy, with Ganges water sprinkled, ho!
s. f. The slough (of a snake, &c.).
adj. & s.m. A little, somewhat
subs: adj. & s.m. Little-knowing; — one who knows but little, a person of limited knowledge.

ety: prob. old P. kandīl arabicized; and akin to Lat. candela
def: s.f. A candle; — a candlestick; a lamp-stand; lantern; shade; sconce; candelabrum; chandelier.
more: Eng. candle. n. f. A Chinese lantern.
s. f. 1. A candle. 2. Lantern, chandelier, shade.

def: s. f. Parsimony.
s.f. Parsimony, niggardliness, stinginess.
= P کنگاش kingāsh, s.f.(?) Advice, counsel; deliberation.
= P کنگاج kingāj or kangāj, s.f.(?) Advice, counsel; deliberation.
